description: Bump all platform packages and plugins from 0.0.73 to 0.0.74, change all publishConfig.access to 'public', publish all packages to npm as public, and rebuild plugins with updated lock files.
qualityChecks: npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run test

userStories.title: Change publishConfig.access to public and bump version to 0.0.74
userStories.description: Change publishConfig.access from 'restricted' to 'public' in all platform packages and all plugins. Add publishConfig to packages missing it. Then bump all version references from 0.0.73 to 0.0.74. Build and verify.
acceptanceCriteria: All 7 platform package.json files have publishConfig.access set to 'public'
All 104 plugin package.json files have publishConfig.access set to 'public'
All 7 platform package.json files have version 0.0.74
All 104 plugin package.json files have version 0.0.74 with SDK/tools dep ranges ^0.0.74
No hardcoded '0.0.73' version strings remain in platform/ TypeScript source
userStories.notes:
This story has two parts: (A) make all packages public, (B) bump versions. Do both before committing.

## Part A: Change publishConfig.access to 'public'

### Platform packages (6 have publishConfig with 'restricted', 1 is missing it entirely)

Change `"access": "restricted"` to `"access": "public"` in:
- platform/shared/package.json (line 20-22: `"publishConfig": { "access": "restricted" }`)
- platform/plugin-sdk/package.json (line 16-18)
- platform/mcp-server/package.json (line 15-17)
- platform/plugin-tools/package.json (line 15-17)
- platform/cli/package.json (line 21-23)
- platform/create-plugin/package.json (line 12-14)

ADD publishConfig to the one package missing it:
- platform/browser-extension/package.json — currently has NO publishConfig block. Add `"publishConfig": { "access": "public" }` (add it after the "version" field)

### Plugins (96 have 'restricted', 7 already have 'public', 1 is missing publishConfig)

Use a script to bulk-update all 104 plugins:

```bash
for dir in plugins/*/; do
  pkg="$dir/package.json"
  python3 -c "
import json, sys
with open(sys.argv[1]) as f:
    d = json.load(f)
if 'publishConfig' not in d:
    # Find position to insert (after 'version' key)
    d['publishConfig'] = {'access': 'public'}
else:
    d['publishConfig']['access'] = 'public'
with open(sys.argv[1], 'w') as f:
    json.dump(d, f, indent=2)
    f.write('\n')
" "$pkg"
done
```

Plugins that already have `"access": "public"` (7 of them): homedepot, instacart, npm, starbucks, terraform-cloud, tripadvisor, yelp — these are already correct, the script is idempotent.

Plugin missing publishConfig entirely: zendesk — the script adds it.

The remaining 96 plugins have `"access": "restricted"` — the script changes them to `"public"`.

After the script, verify:
```bash
grep -l '"restricted"' plugins/*/package.json | wc -l  # Must be 0
grep -l '"restricted"' platform/*/package.json | wc -l  # Must be 0
```

## Part B: Bump versions from 0.0.73 to 0.0.74

Load the bump-version skill: read `.claude/skills/bump-version/__SKILL__.md` and follow Step 1 through Step 4 exactly.

Summary of version changes:
1. Update `"version": "0.0.73"` to `"version": "0.0.74"` in all 7 platform package.json files
2. Update `"version": "0.0.73"` to `"version": "0.0.74"` in platform/browser-extension/manifest.json
3. Update `"version": "0.0.73"` to `"version": "0.0.74"` AND `"@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk": "^0.0.73"` to `"^0.0.74"` AND `"@opentabs-dev/plugin-tools": "^0.0.73"` to `"^0.0.74"` in all 104 plugin package.json files
4. Run `npm install --package-lock-only` at repo root
5. Scan for hardcoded '0.0.73' in platform/**/*.ts
6. Run `npm run build` and `npm run type-check`

Commit everything with message: `bump version to 0.0.74 and make all packages public`

Constraints:
- Do NOT bump the docs/ version — it is independent
- Do NOT modify the root package.json — it has no version field
- Do NOT change any code logic — this is version + access config only
- Plugin packages use `^0.0.74` semver ranges, NOT exact versions

userStories.title: Publish all platform packages and plugins to npm as public
userStories.description: Publish all 7 platform packages to npm with public access in dependency order. Then rebuild all plugins and publish each plugin to npm. Commit plugin lock file updates.
acceptanceCriteria: All 7 platform packages are published to npm at 0.0.74 (verify with npm view)
All 104 plugins are published to npm at 0.0.74 (spot-check 5 with npm view)
All plugin package-lock.json files reference the published 0.0.74 versions
npm view @opentabs-dev/shared@0.0.74 shows access as public
npm view @opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-slack@0.0.74 shows the package exists
userStories.notes:
This story publishes everything to npm. US-001 must be complete first (versions bumped, access set to public, build passing).

## Step 1: Verify npm auth

```bash
npm whoami  # Must return an account with write access to @opentabs-dev
```

## Step 2: Publish platform packages

Publish in strict dependency order. Since all packages now have `publishConfig.access: 'public'`, `npm publish` will use public access automatically:

```bash
cd platform/shared && npm publish
cd platform/browser-extension && npm publish
cd platform/mcp-server && npm publish
cd platform/plugin-sdk && npm publish
cd platform/plugin-tools && npm publish
cd platform/cli && npm publish
cd platform/create-plugin && npm publish
```

After publishing, verify:
```bash
npm view @opentabs-dev/shared@0.0.74 version
npm view @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk@0.0.74 version
npm view @opentabs-dev/cli@0.0.74 version
```

If any publish fails, retry after 10 seconds (registry propagation delay).

## Step 3: Rebuild all plugins

Run `npm run build:plugins` — this installs published platform packages from the registry and builds each plugin.

If any plugin fails with ETARGET (version not found on registry), wait 30 seconds and retry. The npm registry has propagation delays.

## Step 4: Publish all plugins

Publish each plugin to npm. Since plugins have `publishConfig.access: 'public'`, `npm publish` uses public access:

```bash
for dir in plugins/*/; do
  name=$(basename "$dir")
  echo "Publishing $name..."
  (cd "$dir" && npm publish) || echo "FAILED: $name"
done
```

If a plugin fails to publish, note it and retry after 10 seconds. Some plugins may already be published at this version if they were previously public — that's fine, `npm publish` will error with 'cannot publish over previously published version' which is safe to ignore.

After publishing, spot-check:
```bash
npm view @opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-slack@0.0.74 version
npm view @opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-github@0.0.74 version
npm view @opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-discord@0.0.74 version
npm view @opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-notion@0.0.74 version
npm view @opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-netflix@0.0.74 version
```

## Step 5: Commit plugin lock files

Commit all updated plugin lock files:
```bash
git add plugins/*/package-lock.json
git commit -m 'update plugin lock files for 0.0.74'
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT change publishConfig.access values — they were already set to 'public' in US-001
- Do NOT change any code — only publish and commit lock files
- The publish MUST happen before build:plugins, because plugins install from the npm registry
- If a plugin was never published before, `npm publish` with `publishConfig.access: 'public'` will create it as a public scoped package
